page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory-intensive operations, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and a small-block cached read memory bandwidth of 1099.5 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is also strong at 31.65 GB/sec. Other compute benchmarks, such as Geekbench single-core (1547.0) and multi-core (9314.0) scores, fall into the average range. For database workloads, the instance processes 18.03 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a PassMark database score of 6679.37. Large-model LLM inference shows limitations, with prompt processing for a 7B model rated as weak at 27.64 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 11.86 tokens/sec.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32:1 and high-frequency Intel Xeon processor, the x2iezn.4xlarge offers a specialized resource profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive memory footprints per core without needing proportional scaling of vCPU counts. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all disk I/O depends on network-attached storage, which is a tradeoff for storage-heavy workloads. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, large in-memory databases like Redis, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, delivering an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per physical core. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at 4.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides high-frequency compute capabilities alongside its massive memory footprint. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external connectivity. Performance benchmarks show strong memory bandwidth and compression speeds, while general compute and database operations perform in the average tier. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without a corresponding increase in compute cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual server delivering 512 GB of RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 16 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong memory performance with 1099.5 GB/sec cached read bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
